_provenance: V1.4 phase-7 voice pack (V1_4_halina_meeting_research.md). Session 27 (2026-05-13): shared intro factored into client_meeting_intro with chain:true options. Session 29 (2026-05-13): restructured into trust-meter system — three monolithic branch states replaced by round-based structure (intro → r0_response → r1 → r1_response → r2 → r2_response → close → optional reveal). trust_delta on each choice increments chapter1.halina_trust; tier thresholds gate two-variant Halina responses per round. High trust (≥ 5 total) unlocks the landlord-intimidation reveal post-close.
_scope: halina.json owns the Beat 8 client meeting. Trust meter: halina_trust (int), halina_r0/r1/r2_done (bool), halina_r1/r2_choice (string re-fire guards), halina_close_done (bool), landlord_tip_received (bool). Bonus evidence: r0_technical=lease_1962_inheritance_1987, r0_high=wojcik_witness_statement, r0_blunt/r1_high=return_to_sender_slip, r2_high=landlord_contact. Chapter-4 corridor sighting out of scope (Ch4 pack).

states._comment: Shared intro: Asia announces, Halina enters, round of introductions, Murrow hands off. Options (chain:true) present Cula's three opening lines. trust_delta applied before chain re-fires, so the r0_response tier check sees the updated halina_trust immediately.
lines: Good morning. Halina Sikorska. I've been told you can help.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Good morning, Mrs. Sikorska. Dr. A. Cula. Please, sit. Thank you for coming in.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: This is Mr. Crab and Mr. Whimsy. They have read the file. You know Mr. Murrow.

lines: Mr. Murrow. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska.

lines.speaker: whimsy
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska — a pleasure. Forgive the assembly. The file deserves it.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The notice and the docket are in the file. Dr. A. Cula will lead.

choices.text: How are you holding up? The last three weeks...
I wanted to ask you about the notice. When was it dated?
Please tell us about the lease history. How far back does it run?

states._comment: Fires when Cula opened sympathetically (trust ≥ 2). Halina opens up: personal history, long tenancy, Mrs. Wójcik's offer to testify. Sets bonus_evidence = wojcik_witness_statement.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska, how are you holding up? The last three weeks must've been tough...

lines: Thank you for asking. The matter has occupied more of my attention than I would have chosen. I have done what was needed.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: How long have you lived in this apartment.

lines: My father had the lease before me. He worked on the railways. He was given the apartment when he was assigned to Warsaw, in nineteen sixty-two. I inherited in nineteen eighty-seven, after his death. I have lived there since.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And you have been there continuously.

lines: Continuously. My husband moved in in seventy-four, while my father still held the lease. He died in two thousand and nine. My son is in Wrocław.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: There is a neighbour you mentioned. Mrs. Wójcik.

lines: Second floor. She has lived there since nineteen ninety-one. She knows me from the corridor. She would sign a statement, if a statement were needed. I asked her two weeks ago. She offered.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: Continuous resident, second floor, since ninety-one. We can take that statement.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: We will. I have what I need from this part.

states._comment: Fires when Cula opened blunt-procedurally. Halina gives the notice sequence first; the prior returned letter becomes the stance's bonus evidence. **2026-05-25 critique F2:** added two observational asides — the young man's apology at the door, and the woman at the landlord's office who read the address back twice — so the procedural recitation carries the texture the high-trust paths use, without breaking the blunt-procedural register.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska. I wanted to ask about the notice first. Date, receipt, and who brought it to you.

lines: The notice is dated the eighth of April. I received it on the twenty-eighth. The young man now living at number seven brought it down. He had opened the first page before realising it was not for him. He apologised at the door.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Number seven is your old number.

lines: Yes. Since the two thousand and fifteen renumbering, my apartment has been number twelve.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Had the landlord used the correct address before.

lines: Yes. In October, a rent statement sent to number seven came back. A woman at his office telephoned me a week later — she could not have been older than my son. She read the address back twice, to be sure, before they resent it to number twelve.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: Returned once, corrected once, then repeated. That is useful.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Prior knowledge in paper form. Paper is less forgetful than people.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Good. We will ask for the return-to-sender slip.

states._comment: Fires when Cula opened technically (trust < 2, technical stance). Halina stays factual: lease chain, renumbering, notice address discrepancy. Sets bonus_evidence = lease_1962_inheritance_1987. **2026-05-25 critique F2:** added one biographical sentence (Halina's father as a railwayman assigned to Warsaw) so the technical recitation grounds in a teacher's-tic detail without breaking register. Matches the 'railways' biographical detail volunteered in client_meeting_r0_response_high.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska. The lease chain. Walk me through it from the beginning.

lines: My father had the lease from nineteen sixty-two. He was a railwayman — they assigned him the apartment when he was moved to Warsaw. I inherited in eighty-seven, after his death. The renewal is from two thousand and nineteen. Number twelve — the post-renumbering address.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the notice.

lines: Number seven. The address as it stood before the renumbering. The building was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen. My apartment has not been number seven for eleven years.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The landlord renewed to the correct address in two thousand and nineteen. He knew the renumbering had occurred. The notice is defective on its face.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The documents. May I see them.

lines: Of course. The original lease, the inheritance paperwork, and the renewal are here.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. The chain is clean.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: It is.

states._comment: Cula's second question round. Three approaches; trust_delta 2/1/0. write_path halina_r1_choice guards re-fire during chain. Chains to r1_response tier states.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: A few more questions.

choices.text: Mrs. Wójcik — is she available to give a statement this week?
The notice — when was it dated, and when did you actually receive it.
The building was renumbered. What year, and what was the old apartment number.

states.trigger: chapter1.halina_met == false && chapter1.halina_r1_choice != '' && chapter1.halina_r1_done == false && chapter1.halina_trust >= 3
states._comment: Halina responds openly: Mrs. Wójcik available Friday, and she volunteers the return-to-sender slip from October — prior knowledge of correct address. Sets bonus_evidence = return_to_sender_slip.
lines: She is at home on Friday afternoon. She will sign whatever is needed. She has been a widow longer than I have been one. She is patient.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the notice. When was it sent, and when did you receive it.

lines: It was dated the eighth of April. I received it on the twenty-eighth. The current resident of number seven brought it down. He had opened the envelope before realising it was not for him. He apologised at the door. He is a kind young man.

There is one other thing. Six months ago, the landlord sent a rent statement to the old address — number seven. It was returned to sender. A woman at his office telephoned me a week later to say the letter had been returned and that they would resend to the correct address. I do not have the slip. But he does, if he kept it.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: The return-to-sender slip. Producible by request. That establishes knowledge of the correct address before the April notice — prior knowledge on the very point of service.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: We will request it.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Good. I have the timeline.

states._comment: Halina gives the notice timeline factually. Nothing additional is volunteered.
lines: The eighth of April. I received it on the twenty-eighth. Twenty days. The current resident of number seven brought it down. He had read the first line before realising it was not for him.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the building renumbering.

lines: Two thousand and fifteen. My apartment is now number twelve. The notice was addressed to number seven — my address before the renumbering.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And the landlord would have known.

lines: He ought to have known. The renewal is from two thousand and nineteen. It shows number twelve.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. I have the timeline.

states._comment: Cula's third question round. Three approaches; trust_delta 2/1/0. write_path halina_r2_choice guards re-fire during chain. Chains to r2_response tier states.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: One more area.

choices.text: Has the landlord been in contact with you directly — outside of the formal notices.
The cooperative office — is the original lease chain accessible before Thursday.
The renewal agreement of two thousand and nineteen. Does it show the post-renumbering address.

states.trigger: chapter1.halina_met == false && chapter1.halina_r2_choice != '' && chapter1.halina_r2_done == false && chapter1.halina_trust >= 4
states._comment: Halina, now trusting, discloses the landlord's personal visit in March — 'think carefully about your situation.' She hadn't mentioned it; she wasn't sure it was relevant. Intimidation plant for Ch4. Sets bonus_evidence = landlord_contact.
lines: Yes. Once.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Tell me.

lines: In March. Before the notice. He came to the building himself. He knocked on my door. He said he hoped I was comfortable and that I should think carefully about my situation. He was not rude. He left before I could answer.
I did not know whether it was relevant to mention.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: It is relevant.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: March. Before the April notice. Prior contact on the subject of possession. Document it.

lines.speaker: crab
lines.text: If he came in person, the building may have a door camera. The cooperative office can confirm installation date.

lines: I believe there is a camera. They installed it two years ago.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you. You have given us something we did not have.

states._comment: Halina confirms the cooperative chain and renewal address. Nothing personal is volunteered.
lines: The cooperative office is open weekday mornings. The original chain is accessible. I can be there tomorrow if needed.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: We will not need it unless the landlord answers on the underlying tenancy. He will not. The procedural defect ends the matter at the motion.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The renewal of two thousand and nineteen. The address.

lines: Number twelve. As it has stood since the renumbering.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Good. The chain is clean.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: It is.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out (F4/F5):** Beat 8 biographical dwell picker. Scaffolded from cula.json::cula_b8_dwell_options with the apartment option dropped (DUPLICATIVE per recon — biographical content already in client_meeting_r0_response_high). Two remaining design-call dwells (referral + taught) authored per Piotr's 2026-05-25 decision. State-level speaker:'cula'. Ordered BEFORE client_meeting_close so picker wins while state_choice is empty; once both dwells fire, picker shows no remaining choices and player advances to close. Soft issue: no hard guard against re-firing if runner doesn't respect once:true on the reply states — match the pattern used for Pig/Murrow/Crab/Whimsy dwells.
states.speaker: cula
lines: [Before the close: one more biographical question.]

choices.text: And how did you come to find us? Was there anyone else you were speaking with?
I take it from how you have laid out the folder that you have done a lot of careful work in your life. What did you teach, before retirement?

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out:** Beat 8 dwell — *How did you come to find us?* Per cula.json _comment: 'plants Murrow's reputation outside the firm.' Halina-bible reference: clients of this kind would mention a community channel (a neighbour, a tenant union, a parish referral, a former colleague). Cula's prompt + TODO for Halina's reply (Piotr to author).

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: And how did you come to find us? Was there anyone else you were speaking with?

lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Halina's reply. Voice: retired-teacher register, one observational aside. The plant: Mr. Murrow has a reputation outside the firm — a neighbour, a former student, a parish, a tenant association mentioned him. Do not over-explain the channel; one specific detail (the person's name or role) is enough. Pays back in Ch4 retraversal.]

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out:** Beat 8 dwell — *What did you teach?* Per cula.json _comment: 'Halina's reply names Polish language and literature for 38 years and may produce a literary reference.' Pairs with the Iwaszkiewicz reference now in client_meeting_epigram (authored 2026-05-25 per critique F2). Cula's prompt + TODO for Halina's reply (Piotr to author). If a writer is named here, it should NOT be Iwaszkiewicz (avoid double-landing the same author); Polish-literature canon has plenty of room — Tuwim, Konwicki, Szymborska, or a poet she taught to a specific cohort.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: I take it from how you have laid out the folder that you have done a lot of careful work in your life. What did you teach, before retirement?

lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Halina's reply. Voice: retired-teacher register, biographical. Names Polish language and literature; 38 years; one specific writer or text she taught (NOT Iwaszkiewicz — he lands in the epigram state). One sentence of teacher's-tic: noticing what a sentence is doing, naming a register, the kind of class-of-students observation a teacher might make.]

states.trigger: chapter1.halina_met == false && chapter1.halina_r2_done == true && chapter1.halina_close_done == false
states._comment: **2026-05-25 critique F9:** 26-line monolith split into three sequential states. This one (close) carries the fee/retention transaction + Pig's interruption + Murrow's redirect. Cardiologist plant moves to client_meeting_cardiologist_plant. Literary epigram + farewells move to client_meeting_epigram. Hennessy retainer phone reference cut (per critique F9 — Hennessy has no setup anywhere in committed Ch1); replaced with printer + rent-envelope callbacks to established Ch1 firm details. Sets client_fee_agreed; the other flags move to the discrete plant states so each plant lands with breath.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The current tenancy paperwork. The defective notice. The court docket. Mr. Murrow has the file.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: We will file the motion to set aside on the ground of invalid service. The hearing will be rescheduled. You will not need to be present at the motion itself; you may need to be present at the rescheduled hearing, depending on what the landlord does next.

lines: Thank you. I will be available either way.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The fee for the motion and the rescheduled hearing is five thousand. Fixed. It includes the filing, the appearance, and any drafting necessary if the landlord answers.

lines: Five thousand. I have the money. I will transfer it before Friday.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The retention is ready for signature. Two signatures, here and here. I will witness.

lines: Thank you.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: Mr. Murrow. The printer is throwing red lights again. Third time this morning. Where is Asia.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: And the rent envelope from upstairs has reappeared in the in-tray, addressed correctly this time —

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Mr. Pig. A moment. The meeting room.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: The printer. Now.

lines: I gather Mr. Pig is the senior partner.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: He is.

lines: I gather the firm is busy.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: My apologies, Mrs. Sikorska. The printer is a long-running matter.

lines: Of course.

states.trigger: chapter1.client_fee_agreed == true && chapter1.cardiologist_plant_landed == false && chapter1.halina_close_done == false
states._comment: **2026-05-25 critique F9:** cardiologist plant extracted from client_meeting_close as a discrete short state. Per story.txt Beat 8: framed neutrally — no italics, no clutched chest, no Cula reaction line. Single Halina line, then dismiss. The next state's trigger (the epigram) waits on cardiologist_plant_landed. The discrete state is the breath the plant needs to be heard rather than skimmed past.
lines: I will need to reschedule with my cardiologist again. He is patient with me.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 critique F9:** literary epigram extracted from client_meeting_close, now a discrete short state. The Iwaszkiewicz reference (flagged for phase-7 voice work in story.txt Beat 8 and never written until now per critique F2) lands as a teacher's biographical aside — not a fabricated direct quote; Halina names that she taught his stories for thirty-eight years and only now believes them. Cula's response (previously orphaned in cula.json::cula_b8_literary_epigram_reaction per F4 recon) is inlined here. Sets halina_met + halina_close_done; downstream client_meeting_reveal and halina_post_meeting_decoy_incapacity_cold continue to gate on halina_met.
lines: You go to a lawyer like you go to a doctor: too late.
It is something Iwaszkiewicz might have written. I taught his stories for thirty-eight years. I did not believe them until this year.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: I will try to make the visit worth the timing, Mrs. Sikorska.

lines: Until Friday.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Until Friday. Thank you, Mrs. Sikorska.

lines: Thank you, Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy. Mr. Murrow.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Murrow. Archive Room?

states._comment: v4 addition 2026-05-16: 'Incapacity blunder gets cold in-fiction pushback from Halina/Crab/Murrow.' Fires when Cula went back to Crab post-meeting, picked D5, and re-engages Halina before court. In-fiction channel: Mr. Murrow has notified the client. Halina's voice stays measured; the cold is in the brevity and in the address-form Cula uses to her, not in volume. once:true so the state doesn't loop on each re-engage. **2026-05-19 critique F10:** reordered to fire BEFORE client_meeting_reveal so the behavioural rebuke lands before the earned disclosure when both qualify. **2026-05-25 critique F2:** original closer ('I am not too old to be served with a letter. I am too inconvenient to be served with the right letter.') was a two-clause antithesis that read as Murrow's voice on Halina's mouth; rewritten as three short sentences a 71-year-old former Polish-literature teacher would actually speak, with the substantive point preserved (case is about service, not capacity) and the landlord's countersignature named directly.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Murrow rang. He read me the proposed argument on the question of my age.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska —

lines: I am seventy-one. I have lived in that flat since nineteen eighty-seven. I am not the reason the letter went to the wrong door. The landlord knew the right door — he countersigned for it in two thousand and nineteen.
Please withdraw it. The case is not about whether I can read.

states._comment: High-trust reveal (fires once after close if total trust ≥ 5). Halina catches Cula alone: landlord came to her door in February, before the notice — 'think carefully about your situation.' She thought it was manners. It was not. Seeds the Ch4 landlord-intimidation thread and strengthens the cardiologist plant. Must be ordered before post_meeting_ch1 so it takes priority when halina_met == true. **2026-05-19 critique F10:** reordered to fire AFTER halina_post_meeting_decoy_incapacity_cold so the rebuke (behavioural correction) lands before the reveal (earned disclosure) on the rare conjoint-condition path.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. A moment, if you have one.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Of course.

lines: I should have told you this inside. In February — before the notice — the landlord came to the building himself. He knocked on my door. He said he hoped I was comfortable. He said I should think carefully about my situation. He smiled when he said it. He left before I could answer properly.
I thought at the time it was just his manner. People in his position sometimes have that manner.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: February. That is two months before the April notice.

lines: Yes.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: You were right to tell me. If anything else comes back to you — anything at all — bring it to Mr. Murrow. He will pass it to me.

lines: Yes. I will.
Until Friday, Dr. A. Cula.
Thank you again, Dr. A. Cula. I will be available if anything is needed before Friday.
states._comment_ch4_scope: TODO: Chapter-4 corridor sighting (β reappearance) is out of scope for this file. Wire a ch4-triggered state when the Ch4 voice pack is authored. The corridor sighting is approximately five lines; see narrative_revision/bibles/halina_sikorska.md §Chapter 4 for the line-shape inventory.
